summary.intent: Refactor Gossamer from an Electron desktop app into a VS Code extension, then iteratively add features including session detail view, checkpoint sidebar, search, MCP server, image support, and agent-awareness.
summary.outcome: A fully working VS Code extension was built with sessions list, session detail tabs, native checkpoint tree view, search via QuickPick, MCP server embedded at /mcp, image rendering, session naming, agent-aware commands, and file watcher-based live updates.

repo: Each repo in ~/.gossamer/config.json has its own SQLite database at a configurable dbPath — there is no shared global DB
The server runs from gossamer-testing/dist/serve.js (the testing worktree), not from the main repo dist/ — any rebuild must target the worktree
Entire.io writes session data to the entire/checkpoints/v1 git branch; shadow sessions are written live to a separate shadow branch during active sessions
Custom-title events (from /rename) only exist in ~/.claude/projects/<repo>/*.jsonl — they are NOT present in checkpoint or shadow branches and must be read separately
Session color chosen in Claude Code is not persisted to disk anywhere — unavailable to Gossamer
Agent info (claude-code, codex, gemini-cli, etc.) comes from checkpoint metadata in the DB (CheckpointSessionMetadata.agent)
Images are stored as base64 in full.jsonl files on the checkpoint branch under source.data and source.media_type fields
The LogContent Prisma model lacked imageData/imageMediaType fields; images were silently dropped during indexing until schema was extended
findRepo() uses exact-match on localPath; when session cwd is a subdirectory of the repo root, it fails — requires prefix-matching findRepoForPath()

better-sqlite3 is a native module compiled for system Node.js (MODULE_VERSION 141) but VS Code's extension host uses Electron's Node.js (MODULE_VERSION 140) — must spawn server as child process using system node
acquireVsCodeApi() can only be called once per webview context — must be stored as window.__vscodeApi and reused
The DEFAULT_PORT is 3456, stored in src/config.ts and read by both the server and VS Code extension from ~/.gossamer/config.json

code.finding: Original config only listed extension.ts and GossamerPanel.ts in 'include' — SessionDetailPanel.ts and diffUtils.ts were never compiled, causing silent runtime failures. Fixed by using 'src/**/*.ts' with webview excluded.

Server is a static singleton (GossamerPanel.server, GossamerPanel.serverPort) so it survives panel disposal. minimize() disposes the webview only; restart() kills and respawns. onDidChangeViewState re-sends server_ready when panel becomes visible after a window move.
Must check window.__vscodeApi before calling acquireVsCodeApi() to avoid 'can only be called once' error when the host page script already acquired it.
root and outDir must be absolute paths (using resolve(__dirname, ...)) — relative paths resolve against CWD when run via npm workspace -w flag, not the package directory.

Mantine ScrollArea viewport ref is null at [] dependency useEffect time — scroll position must be checked inline at effect-fire time, not via a separate scroll listener. Auto-scroll uses initialLoadDone ref to avoid jumping on open.

customTitleCache is populated by scanning ~/.claude/projects/<hash>/sessions/*.jsonl for custom-title events at startup and after each shadow poll. mtime-based caching avoids re-parsing unchanged files.

resourceUri on TreeItems requires absolute paths — relative paths from filesTouched must be joined with the repo root. The onDidChangeActiveWebviewPanel event (via onDidChangeViewState on each panel) is needed to keep the tree in sync when switching tabs.
Uses TextDocumentContentProvider with gossamer-diff: URI scheme to serve before/after content from memory — avoids temp files and produces inherently read-only documents in vscode.diff.

workflow: Git worktrees cannot checkout the same branch in two places — must create a new branch (testing) from the source branch (mcp) for the worktree
When a VS Code extension is loaded from a worktree, all server rebuilds must happen in the worktree, not the main repo
VS Code webview Content Security Policy blocks data: URIs for images by default — must explicitly add img-src data: https: http: to the CSP header
VS Code Timeline API is file-centric (provideTimeline(uri)) and doesn't support nested children — unsuitable for checkpoint grouping; native TreeView is better
Chord keybindings (Ctrl+K G) don't fire when a webview has focus — panel toggle must also be triggerable from within the webview itself
The MCP StreamableHTTPServerTransport requires sessionIdGenerator: undefined for stateless mode
npm run build -w <workspace> requires the exact workspace name from package.json 'name' field, not the directory name

friction: Server running from gossamer-testing worktree stale dist/ caused multiple debugging cycles — imageData appeared in main repo but not in the running server
tsconfig.extension.json missing files caused SessionDetailPanel.ts and diffUtils.ts to be silently omitted from compilation with no build error
Multiple context compactions mid-session caused repeated re-derivation of project state
Prisma db push --accept-data-loss required for adding nullable columns to SQLite; one DB (entireio_cli.db) had FTS table conflict requiring direct ALTER TABLE
Mantine ScrollArea viewport ref timing caused auto-scroll behavior to be unreliable — required three separate fix attempts
acquireVsCodeApi() called-once constraint not obvious; caused runtime error after adding minimize button with inline script
Vite's relative root path resolved against CWD when invoked via npm workspace, causing 'Cannot resolve entry module index.html' error
Content provider approach for diffs was fragile (URI encoding issues) and had to be abandoned for temp files, then later replaced with in-memory provider
Accidentally made Electron app changes when user asked about minimizing — VS Code context was lost mid-session
Custom-title events (session rename) not in checkpoint/shadow branches — required separate JSONL scan approach with mtime caching
open_items: Session color data: Claude Code doesn't persist color choices to disk, so Gossamer cannot display them
Toggle panel keybinding (Ctrl+K G) doesn't fire when webview has focus — needs an in-webview fallback beyond the minimize button
Named sessions (customTitle) need to be visually distinguished from unnamed (slug-only) ones in the sessions list — partially done but styling may need refinement
Agent CLI mapping in AGENT_CLI constant may need expansion as new AI coding agents emerge
Old sessions indexed before imageData schema change need re-sync (Sync button) to show images — no automatic backfill for new installs
The gossamer-testing worktree branch (testing) diverges from mcp and needs periodic merges to stay current
MCP stdio wrapper (dist/mcp.js) requires knowing the port upfront — the install-mcp.mjs script handles this but users must re-run it if the port changes
Session resume command for unknown agents falls back to 'claude --resume' — should surface a warning or prompt
Onboarding flow does not verify Entire.io CLI is actually installed, only that .entire/settings.json exists
